item.title: George Harrison
item.description: EraBritish Invasion (with the Beatles, 1963–1970); solo, 1968–2002InstrumentLead guitar, slide guitar, sitar, vocalsGenresRockPop rockFolk rockBandsThe BeatlesTraveling WilburysListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify George Harrison played as if notes cost money.

item.title: PRODUCERS
item.description: The figures who shape how a record sounds — the people whose choices about arrangement, room, and tape are audible in the finished track.

item.title: SESSION MUSICIANS
item.description: The uncredited players who cut the records — the house bands and hired hands whose work is on the hits without their names on the labels.

item.title: SONGWRITERS
item.description: The writers behind the records — from the staff professionals who supplied other people’s hits to the singer-songwriters who performed their own.

item.title: The Beach Boys
item.description: Era1961–present (primary period: 1963–1970)GenresSurf rockVocal surfBaroque popSunshine popPsychedelic popProgressive popPop rockSceneHawthorne / HollywoodCaliforniaListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The Beach Boys are one instrument made of five throats.

item.title: The Beatles
item.description: EraBritish Invasion (1963–1970)GenresPop rockPsychedelic popArt rockSceneMerseybeatMovementBritish beat boom Playlist: FAB RYM · Apple Music · Spotify One chord opens A Hard Day’s Night, and no two musicians have ever agreed how to name it.

item.title: Electric Texas blues
item.description: Period1942–presentLocationDallas and Houston, later AustinInfluencesAcoustic Texas bluesJazzJump bluesLinksRYM A single-string line that phrases like a horn.

item.title: EXECUTIVES
item.description: The people whose decisions determined what got made — the label heads, A&R men, and dealmakers who chose what to fund, sign, and release.

item.title: LABELS
item.description: The record labels with an identity of their own — the houses whose roster, sound, and judgment shaped what their era heard.
